changes.reason: Batch G isolated no-0DTE fallback refinement. STALE_0DTE breakout fallback has weak efficiency: 2 trades, only $71 PnL, $40.5/exposure hour, avg MAE -210.6, and 52.6 minute median hold. Raising the ES VWAP slope floor from 0.08 to the grid value 0.10 tests whether stale/no-0DTE breakout fallback should require stronger ES trend confirmation without touching VALID_0DTE profiles.

The weak stale breakout fallback is not worth admitting unless ES structure is clean. Moving the no-0DTE breakout structure floor from 4 to 5 is profile-specific and data-state scoped, avoiding option-dependent profile leakage into stale or missing 0DTE states.

Current no-0DTE breakout fallback uses a 3 point base TP while stale entries have long holds and low realized PnL. Raising to 4 keeps the EXPIRED_0DTE convex winner potential while testing whether marginal stale breakout entries need more payoff to justify exposure.

recommendation.expected_effect: Should improve no-0DTE fallback exposure quality by reducing or repricing the weak STALE_0DTE breakout branch while leaving VALID_0DTE pullback, charm-pin, squeeze, call-wall, and charm-wall edges unchanged. Trade count may drop by up to two stale breakout trades; reject if fallback PnL turns negative, total net profit falls below 98% of run_044, or the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner is lost without a compensating risk-adjusted improvement.
recommendation.label: no-0dte-breakout-quality-holdout
recommendation.rationale: Use Batch G because the latest attribution cleanly separates data states. VALID_0DTE remains the main edge and should be frozen, while no-0DTE fallback is profitable but mixed: EXPIRED_0DTE has one strong breakout winner, pullback stale is modestly positive, and breakout stale is low-efficiency with materially worse MAE. This is a narrow fallback-profile experiment, not a global veto or option-data fail-open change, and it avoids repeating the saturated call-wall and charm-wall take-profit keysets.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes 20 trades and $1315.3 with no slow trades, but direct 1DTE tightening has repeatedly been risky. A companion route may separate pullback/reclaim from clean breakout without weakening breakout_long_1dte.
strategy_hypotheses.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The companion may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or be unreachable under current trigger logic.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL stays >= $1315, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -124.6, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL is measured, and total score is flat or better.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long contributes $640.5 on only 2 trades and owns the -655.2 worst MAE; the worst tail is also a $546 convex winner, so static pruning is fragile.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Can overfit a two-trade profile and cut rare convex winners unless protected fast-winner retention is enforced.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: call_wall_acceptance_long entry with wide target or extended ES-VWAP that fails to make sustained early progress before adverse expansion.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce worst MAE below -655.2 while retaining >=70% call-wall PnL, total net profit >=98% of leader, and slow_12h remains 0.
